label: Bone
quick_take: Bone cancer has small absolute rates (median around 1) and the signal-to-noise is poor. Most registry-level outliers sit on single-digit case counts. The few findings kept here are either built on large aggregate case counts (USA SEER/NPCR) or are flagged as provisional.

findings.title: Israel Jews born in Africa / Asia women vol V: ASR 6.49, 8.8× the world median
findings.text: The top spatial outlier for this site by a wide margin. Israel aggregate n=38→255 cases, ASR ~1 throughout; the 6.49 reading refers to a narrower Sephardic subset in a single early volume and may not be fully auditable from the current CI5 2.0 plot. Pop_warning Israel vol V. Keep as a historical curiosity worth checking against the original vol V tables.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Israel Jews born in Africa / Asia women vol V: ASR 6.49

findings.title: USA aggregate (SEER + NPCR) male age curves are bimodal
findings.text: With the peak at age band 18 (85+). The low-age (adolescent) hump is visible but small relative to the chondrosarcoma/osteosarcoma-in-older-adults bulk. The USA bimodal pattern across vol X–XII may reflect how chondrosarcomas of the pelvis are being counted — large N (>6000 cases per vol) makes this easily visualisable.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — USA aggregate (SEER + NPCR) male age curves are bimodal

findings.text: Small numerators almost everywhere; rank bone findings by number of cases, not percentage change. Claims about Hawaii-Chinese men (n=0–9), Italian Ferrara/Sassari/Barletta M:F ratios (n=5–8), Karunagappally women U-shape (n=1→12), Xiangfu single-volume 5.88, and the USA-Black vs USA-other 10× historical gap all failed the ≥20-case threshold and were cut from the headline list.
The interpretation of "bone cancer" in CI5 mixes osteosarcoma, chondrosarcoma, Ewing, and (historically, in some registries) multiple myeloma — sub-type splits would be needed for a real writeup.
The bimodal US age curves are likely a registration artefact, not a biological signal.
Consider running all sarcoma-containing sites together (bone + soft-tissue under "other") to separate real geographic signal from coding drift.
